lessons.title: One to Five
lessons.topic: Counting to 5 and sounds of K and L

steps.instruction: Watch the video and answer these questions!

questions.question_text: What number comes after one?
questions.hint: It's the next number you say after one.

questions.question_text: Which one is an animal?
What number do we start with?
questions.hint: It's the first one we say.

steps.title: The Kite and the Lions
steps.text: Look! One bright kite flies. Two lions play under it. It is one kite. It is two lions. They are happy!

steps.explanation: We use 'It is' to say what something is.

steps.audio_text: Wow, look! I see one big lion sitting under a tree. And look up! There are two kites flying in the blue sky.

questions.question: How many kites do you see?
How many lions are under the tree?

questions.question_text: Fill in the blank: It is ___ kite. (Vocabulary and Grammar question)
What animal did we learn about? (Vocabulary question)
How many kites do you see if you add two more kites to one kite? (Counting comprehension)
True or False: 'Look' starts with the letter L.
Which sound does the letter 'K' start? (Sound recognition)

lessons.title: Six to Ten
lessons.topic: Counting to 10 and the sound of M

steps.instruction: Watch the video and answer these questions!

questions.question_text: How many lions are sitting under the tree?
questions.hint: Count the lions sitting together near the big tree.

questions.question_text: What color is the biggest lion in the video?
questions.hint: Look for the lion with the most golden fur.

questions.question_text: What sound do the lions make in the video?
questions.hint: Listen for the loud sound that lions are famous for.

items.example: How many mouths do you have?

steps.title: Monkeys and Mouths
steps.text: It is morning in the jungle. I see six monkeys swinging in the trees. Look! It is a mouth of a big monkey. There are ten bananas on the ground. How many monkeys? Let's count them.

steps.explanation: Use 'I see' to say what you look at.

steps.audio_text: I have a monkey puppet. It is my favorite toy! I see him jumping on my bed.

questions.question: What is the monkey puppet doing?

questions.question_text: I see ___ monkeys. (Complete the sentence)
What sound does a monkey make? (Vocabulary question)
Complete the sentence: 'I see a __.'
Which number comes after six?
True or False: The word 'ten' has the sound of 'M'.

lessons.title: How Many?
lessons.topic: Asking about quantity and sounds of N and O

steps.instruction: Watch the video and answer these questions!

questions.question_text: What number do you hear first in the song?
What begins with the letter 'O'?
questions.hint: It's an animal that lives in the sea.

questions.question_text: How many apples do you see in the video?
questions.hint: Count all the red fruits in the basket.

items.example: How many arms? It is eight.

steps.title: Animal Adventure
steps.text: Look at the sea. It is one octopus. How many monkeys? It is nine. My nose smells the sea. I see a big orange fish!

steps.explanation: Use 'How many' to ask about numbers.

items.sentence: How many apple do you see?
How many book on the table?

steps.audio_text: I have a pet octopus. It has eight arms! Next to it, there are nine oranges. Can you see them?

questions.question: How many arms does the octopus have?

questions.question_text: How many arms does an octopus have?
What letter does 'nose' start with? (Vocabulary question)
How many oranges are there in the basket?
Complete the sentence: How many ___ do you see?
True or False: The octopus is blue.

steps.instruction: Watch the video and answer these questions!

questions.question_text: Which picture starts with the letter 'L'?
questions.hint: Think of a big cat with a loud roar!

questions.question_text: Which picture is something that lights up a room?
questions.hint: It sits on a table and helps you see in the dark.

questions.question_text: Which picture is something that grows on a tree?
questions.hint: They're green and rustle in the wind.

steps.title: Sam's Playground
steps.text: Hi! I am Sam. I see two slides and five swings. It is fun! I have a red ball. How many swings? Five!

questions.question: How many swings does Sam see?

steps.audio_text: Look, I have five bananas. My brother has three. How many bananas do we have together?

questions.question: How many bananas do I have?
How many bananas do we have together?

steps.prompt: Say: How many cats do you see?

questions.question_text: How many apples are there if you have three and I have four?
It is ___ o'clock. (Grammar question)
Which start letter comes after 'K'?
True or False: Plurals mean more than one.
